text:
Centre for Epilepsy

Neurosciences Division

Clinic Date: 08 May 2017

Dr Aisha Karim
Consultant Neurologist
Centre for Epilepsy
King’s College Hospital, Denmark Hill, London SE5 9RS

Dear Dr Aisha Karim,

Mr Amir Rahman, DOB: 12-03-1992, Hospital No: KCH847563 NHS No. 9432175086
Flat 3, 27 Brook Street, London, SE15 4DN

Subject: Parking concession eligibility for clinic attendance

Diagnosis:

Under follow-up in neurology clinic.

Context and Access Needs:

Recent migrant to the UK; occasionally attends with interpreter support. Requires clear guidance on hospital access and costs during appointments.

Parking Concession:

The patient is eligible for parking concessions when attending scheduled neurology appointments at our service. This includes reduced-rate parking for the duration of the appointment and up to 30 minutes post-appointment to allow for check-out and pharmacy collection.

Process:

- On arrival, the patient should take a parking ticket and bring it to the clinic reception for validation.
- Clinic staff will stamp the ticket and issue a concession voucher where required.
- If using the pay-by-phone system, the clinic reception will provide a discount code at check-in.
- Interpreters attending with the patient can also use the same concession for the duration of the appointment.

Eligibility Period:

Concessions apply for all pre-booked neurology clinic visits and urgent nurse-led reviews at our site. They do not apply outside appointment times or for non-clinic visits.

Contact and Support:

For questions about validation or if there are issues at the barrier, the patient should speak to clinic reception. Written guidance is available and can be provided in alternative languages on request.

Management Plan:

- Provide the patient with a printed, easy-read parking guide today.
- Note eligibility flag on the clinic booking system for automatic reminder at check-in.
- Review understanding at next appointment.

Yours sincerely

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: Reviewed the entire letter. It is an administrative note about parking concessions for neurology clinic attendance. There is no mention of seizures, seizure events, or any seizure frequency. No implicit or explicit numeric seizure data is provided. Therefore, per instruction, when seizures are not mentioned at all, the correct label is 'no seizure frequency reference'.
reference: Create a reasonable NHS letter providing information on parking concession eligibility for epilepsy clinic attendees

flaws.description: No seizure frequency referenced in gold
Row marked as not OK in raw dataset
